nodes.description: Aggregate fact table summarizing video engagement by video, learner, and a courserun.
Platform information about the technical system that a given event is executed within the context of.

Slowly Changing Dimension model for course content, including courses, chapters, subsections, problems, videos, and other contents across the Open edX platforms.

Eventually a dimensional model for users from different platforms. It will contain one record for a user that's potentially a part of different platforms where we are able to see they are the same person.

MITx and xPro Program Summary report. A count of all users, enrollments (audit and verified), certificates and program credentials aggregated for each program.

Engagement report by learner, course, courserun, section and subsection. A count of posts replied, posts created, average percent grade, percent problems attempted, number of problems, problems attempted, video duration, estimated time played, and number of page views.
Enrollment detail report for MITxOnline, xPro and edX.org courses. A detailed view of enrollments, course runs, course certificates, orders, and user information for each enrollment.

Learners video engagement - play, pause, seek, or stop video actions from MITx Online, xPro, edX.org, Residential MITx with calculated counts for total video events, distinct videos watched, and max video index.
Problem and grade information that is associated with a particular course run, platform, and learner that is used for cheating detection analysis.
A report that contains student risk probability scores and course grade
AI chatbot interactions from Learn AI application including TutorBot, ResourceRecommendationBot, SyllabusBot and VideoGPTBot. No personal information is included in this report.
A report showing certificates/completions for later roll-up at the organizational level.
A report showing chatbot data for later roll-up at the instructor level.
A report that contains mitxonline video engagement data.
A report that contains enrollment activity metrics
A report that contains learner demographics and cerificate information
A report that contains learner enrollment details with demographic information
A report that contains learner engagement details with problem completion information
A report that contains learner engagement info with problem data
Page engagement report showing user activity at the unit, subsection, and section level across course runs. Joins page engagement facts with course content hierarchy and user information.

Program enrollment report enriched with user demographic data and course completion details. Extends marts__combined_program_enrollment_detail with user profile information, capstone completion indicator, and program completion duration.
Problem engagement detail report showing user performance at the problem level across course runs. Includes attempt counts, grade information, and course content hierarchy.

MITx Online daily course engagement report with platform, course status, and course identification fields. Shows user engagement metrics including course events, problem submissions, video plays, and discussion participation aggregated by date, user, and course run.

Combined video engagement report across all platforms with video counts per chapter. Shows user video watching activity including total videos available in each chapter and how many the user has watched.

Since mitxonline orders always have exactly one line, we can combine the ecommerce_line and ecommnerce_order tables into one intermediate table. This may change at some point in the future (xpro orders have multiple lines). If it does, the unique test on order_id in this model will fail and we will know to change this query and the queries that depend on it

A B2B order is created when a company or other purchaser buys enrollment codes for many of learners through the xpro web form. This creates full price off coupons that learners can redeem for an enrollment
B2B coupons are coupons that allow us to give a discount to companies buying bulk seats for their employees
Coupon assignments made through bulk coupon assignment google sheets. These coupons can be redeemed by users with a specific email for a specific product
This table is used to limit coupons to a specific product. Coupons which do not have a record in this table are redeemable for all products

The ecommerce_couponpaymentversion holds information about a batch of coupons. A coupon payment version has information about the dollar or percentage amount of the discount, the number of available coupons that can be used to redeem the discount, how many times they can be redeemed and the source of the coupons. Coupon groups can be generated internally, for example to allow staff to enroll in courses without paying or marketing to create promos that allow learners to take a percentage or dollar amount off the full course price. Other coupon groups are created when a company buys bulk seats in a course for their employees, which generates a batch of 100% off coupons that learners can use to enroll in a course or program.
A coupon code that can be used to recieve a discount on a course or program
A company that purchases bulk enrollments to xpro courses or programs

The course runs the user selected for the basket items in their basket. If the basket item is a course run, there is one ecommerce_basketrunselection record. If the basket item  is a program, there will be a record for each course run from the program that is selected by the user. Once the user completes the purchase, these records become records in ecommerce_linerunselection

After the dedp micromasters was divided into two tracks the rules for which DEDP program the user can apply 14.009x Good Economics for Hard Times are as follows 'If you completed 14.009x Good Economics for Hard Times before the launch of the Public Policy Track in fall 2023, you can apply the course to the International Development Track indefinitely. However, if you completed 14.009x before fall 2023 and wish to apply it to the Public Policy Track, it cannot be counted towards the International Development Track. In this case, you will need to complete two other International Development Track electives excluding 14.009x) and the three core courses to earn the International Development credential. Starting from fall 2023, completing 14.009x will only be credited towards the Public Policy Track.' Users can recieve both DEDP certificates but only count 14.009x once

This table captures the historical changes made to the edX.org course structure by comparing the new course block to the course block from the previous day. If no changes are detected, no new rows are added to the table. If there are any changes to the course structure, all blocks, including the updated or new ones, are added to the table.

Coupon groups can be generated internally, for example to allow staff to enroll in courses without paying or marketing to create promos that allow learners to take a percentage or dollar amount off the full course price.
